_about: Proov calibration set (Story 3.1, NFR4). ~50 hand-labeled rows — a COMMITTED product artifact, not test-only. Two row kinds: 'claim' rows carry the retrieved 'evidence' + the raw 'recorded_judgment' the live model returned (captured via `python scripts/calibrate.py --live`) and a 'gold_status'; 'citation' rows carry a 'recorded_fetch' (the HTTP/transport outcome) + optional 'recorded_support_status' and a 'gold_flag'. The offline replay (scripts/calibrate.py + tests/test_calibration.py) feeds the recorded_* fields through the REAL deterministic engine (_normalise_judgment / _classify_retrievability / _flag_for) to reproduce a prediction with NO network and NO spend. The set is deliberately HONEST: it seeds a small number of real model errors so measured precision is < 1.0 but >= 0.80 on the flagged classes (proving the bar is a real threshold, not a tautology).

expected.unsupported_precision: 0.833 (5/6) — below 1.0 by one honest false-flag (claim-unsup-fp-01); the meaningful-gate guard
expected.fabricated_precision: 1.00 (4/4) — only definitive 404/410 are flagged fabricated; the precision fix removed the old transient-5xx false positives (now ok)
expected.thin_evidence_unverifiable_rate: 1.00 (8/8) — every thin row resolves to unverifiable (anti-guess guard)
expected.note: The 5 ambiguous citation rows (403/429/503/500/timeout) are gold 'ok'; the OLD code mis-flagged them 'fabricated' (5 false positives). The new 404/410-only rule is what lifts fabricated-flag precision.

rows.claim: The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France.

evidence.title: Eiffel Tower
evidence.snippet: The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France.

evidence.quote: a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France

rows.claim: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

evidence.title: Boiling point
evidence.snippet: At standard atmospheric pressure (sea level), water boils at 100 degrees Celsius.

evidence.quote: water boils at 100 degrees Celsius

rows.claim: The Great Wall of China is located in China.

evidence.title: Great Wall of China
evidence.snippet: The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications built across the historical northern borders of ancient Chinese states.

evidence.quote: built across the historical northern borders of ancient Chinese states

rows.claim: Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level.

evidence.title: Mount Everest
evidence.snippet: Mount Everest is Earth's highest mountain above sea level, located in the Mahalangur Himal sub-range of the Himalayas.

evidence.quote: Earth's highest mountain above sea level

rows.claim: The speed of light in vacuum is about 299,792 kilometres per second.

evidence.title: Speed of light
evidence.snippet: The speed of light in vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 metres per second (about 299,792 km/s).

evidence.quote: 299,792,458 metres per second

rows.claim: The human heart has four chambers.

evidence.title: Heart
evidence.snippet: The human heart has four chambers: two upper atria and two lower ventricles.

evidence.quote: four chambers: two upper atria and two lower ventricles

rows.claim: Photosynthesis occurs in the chloroplasts of plant cells.

evidence.title: Photosynthesis
evidence.snippet: In plants, photosynthesis takes place in chloroplasts, which contain the chlorophyll.

evidence.quote: photosynthesis takes place in chloroplasts

rows.claim: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.

evidence.title: Pacific Ocean
evidence.snippet: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's five oceanic divisions.

evidence.quote: the largest and deepest of Earth's five oceanic divisions

rows.claim: William Shakespeare wrote the play Hamlet.

evidence.title: Hamlet
evidence.snippet: Hamlet is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare sometime between 1599 and 1601.

evidence.quote: a tragedy written by William Shakespeare

rows.claim: The chemical symbol for gold is Au.

evidence.title: Gold
evidence.snippet: Gold is a chemical element with the symbol Au (from Latin aurum) and atomic number 79.

evidence.quote: the symbol Au (from Latin aurum)

rows.claim: The Amazon River is in South America.

evidence.title: Amazon River
evidence.snippet: The Amazon River in South America is the largest river by discharge volume of water in the world.

evidence.quote: The Amazon River in South America

rows.claim: DNA carries genetic information in living organisms.

evidence.title: DNA
evidence.snippet: DNA is a polymer that carries genetic instructions for the development, functioning, growth and reproduction of all known organisms.

evidence.quote: carries genetic instructions for the development, functioning, growth and reproduction

rows.claim: The Moon orbits the Earth.

evidence.title: Moon
evidence.snippet: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite and orbits the Earth at an average distance of 384,400 km.

evidence.quote: Earth's only natural satellite and orbits the Earth

rows.claim: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.

evidence.title: Mercury
evidence.snippet: Mercury is the first planet from the Sun and the smallest in the Solar System.

evidence.quote: the first planet from the Sun

rows.claim: The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world.

evidence.title: Sahara
evidence.snippet: The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world and the third-largest desert overall.

evidence.quote: the largest hot desert in the world

rows.claim: The freezing point of water is 0 degrees Celsius at sea level.

evidence.title: Melting point
evidence.snippet: The melting (and freezing) point of water at standard pressure is 0 degrees Celsius.

evidence.quote: freezing point of water at standard pressure is 0 degrees Celsius

rows.claim: Tokyo is the capital of Japan.

evidence.title: Tokyo
evidence.snippet: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

evidence.quote: the capital and most populous city of Japan

rows.claim: The Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon with the naked eye.

evidence.title: Great Wall of China
evidence.snippet: The claim that the Great Wall is visible from the Moon with the naked eye is a myth; it is not visible from low Earth orbit without aid.

evidence.quote: visible from the Moon with the naked eye is a myth

rows.claim: Humans use only 10 percent of their brains.

evidence.title: Ten percent of the brain myth
evidence.snippet: The ten percent of the brain myth is the false assertion that most humans only use 10 percent of their brains.

evidence.quote: false assertion that most humans only use 10 percent of their brains

rows.claim: The Earth is the center of the solar system.

evidence.title: Heliocentrism
evidence.snippet: Heliocentrism is the astronomical model in which the Earth and planets revolve around the Sun at the center of the Solar System.

evidence.quote: the Earth and planets revolve around the Sun at the center of the Solar System

rows.claim: Goldfish have a memory span of only three seconds.

evidence.title: Goldfish
evidence.snippet: Studies have shown that goldfish have a memory span of at least three months, contradicting the popular three-second myth.

evidence.quote: a memory span of at least three months, contradicting the popular three-second myth

rows.claim: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.

evidence.title: Lightning
evidence.snippet: Lightning can and often does strike the same place repeatedly; tall structures such as the Empire State Building are struck many times per year.

evidence.quote: can and often does strike the same place repeatedly

rows.claim: The Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889.

evidence.title: Eiffel Tower
evidence.snippet: Constructed from 1887 to 1889 as the centerpiece of the 1889 World's Fair, the Eiffel Tower was the tallest man-made structure in the world.

evidence.quote: Constructed from 1887 to 1889

rows.note: HONEST FALSE FLAG: the model mis-read 'Constructed from 1887 to 1889' and flagged the (true) 1889-completion claim unsupported. This is the one unsupported false positive that keeps unsupported-class precision below 1.0 (the meaningful-gate guard).

rows.claim: Acme Corp's revenue grew 23 percent in Q3 2025.

evidence.title: Acme Q3 2025 press release
evidence.snippet: Acme Corp today announced revenue grew 23 percent year-over-year in the third quarter of 2025.

evidence.quote: revenue grew 23 percent year-over-year in the third quarter of 2025

rows.note: Model asserted supported off a single self-published press release; gold is unverifiable (not independently corroborated). A recall miss on unverifiable, not a flagged-class error — does not affect the precision gate.

rows.claim: An unnamed startup raised five million dollars last week.

evidence.quote: raised five million dollars

rows.note: Empty retrieved evidence: the model's cited source is not among the (zero) retrieved sources, so grounding drops it and the supported label downgrades to unverifiable.

rows.claim: The new policy will reduce operating costs by 40 percent.

evidence.title: Policy brief
evidence.snippet: The brief outlines the policy goals but does not quantify the projected cost savings.

evidence.quote: cuts operating costs by 40 percent

rows.note: Label-needs-evidence downgrade: the model cited a source NOT in the retrieved evidence (fabricated grounding), so it is dropped and supported downgrades to unverifiable.

rows.claim: Some assert the board meeting happened on Tuesday.
rows.note: Blank status coerces to unverifiable.

rows.claim: The product may launch in 2026.
rows.note: Unknown status 'maybe' coerces to unverifiable.

rows.claim: There is no record that the old bridge was built in 1850.

evidence.quote: no construction date is recorded

rows.note: Empty evidence with an unsupported label and an ungrounded citation: grounding drops it and unsupported downgrades to unverifiable (precision over recall — no flag on nothing).

rows.claim: The author lives in Berlin.

evidence.title: Author bio
evidence.snippet: The author bio page is currently unavailable.

rows.note: Recorded judgment carried no evidence items, so a supported label has no grounding and downgrades to unverifiable.

rows.claim: The treaty was signed in a private session.

evidence.title: Treaty history
evidence.snippet: Records of the signing session are incomplete.

rows.note: The single cited evidence item has a blank quote, so it is dropped; the supported label then has no grounding and downgrades to unverifiable.

rows.claim: The committee approved the proposal unanimously.

evidence.title: Committee minutes
evidence.snippet: The minutes do not record the vote tally.

evidence.quote: approved unanimously

rows.note: The cited evidence item has a blank source (not in the retrieved set), so it is dropped and the supported label downgrades to unverifiable.

rows.claim: The corner cafe serves the best coffee in town.

evidence.title: Corner Cafe reviews
evidence.snippet: Reviews of the corner cafe are mixed, with praise for the pastries and criticism of the espresso.

evidence.quote: Reviews of the corner cafe are mixed

rows.note: Subjective superlative; the model correctly returns unverifiable even with evidence present (not thin).

rows.claim: The upcoming election results will be close.

evidence.title: Polling summary
evidence.snippet: Polls for the race show the candidates within the margin of error, but the outcome is not yet decided.

evidence.quote: the outcome is not yet decided

rows.note: A prediction about an undecided future event; correctly unverifiable (not thin).

rows.claim: The comet will be visible to the naked eye next month.

evidence.title: Comet forecast
evidence.snippet: Whether the comet brightens enough to be seen without aid depends on outgassing that cannot yet be predicted.

evidence.quote: depends on outgassing that cannot yet be predicted

rows.note: Uncertain future visibility; correctly unverifiable (not thin).
Definitive 404 — the source does not exist. Correctly fabricated.
Definitive 410 Gone. Correctly fabricated.
Definitive 404. Correctly fabricated.
Definitive 410 Gone. Correctly fabricated.
403 Forbidden (paywalled / bot-blocked) — ambiguous, NOT absent. The OLD code mis-flagged this fabricated; the fix maps it to ok (cannot prove fabricated).
429 Too Many Requests — transient. Ambiguous -> ok. Old false fabricated removed.
503 Service Unavailable — momentarily down. Ambiguous -> ok. Old false fabricated removed.
500 Internal Server Error — server fault, not proof of absence. Ambiguous -> ok.
Transport timeout — flaky, not proof of absence. Ambiguous -> ok. Old false fabricated removed.
Retrievable and supports the attached output. ok.
Retrievable and supports. ok.
Retrievable and supports. ok.
Retrievable but the source positively does NOT back the attached output. misattributed.
Retrievable but positively refutes the attached output. misattributed.
Retrievable; support is unconfirmed (judge unverifiable). We do not cry wolf -> ok with support unconfirmed.
